info.title: Agntix API
info.description: Unified API for the Agentix AI platform — agents, chat, voice, telephony, analytics, and more.

201.description: The agent has been successfully created.

get.description: Retrieve pre-built agent templates organized by use case (customer service, sales, support, etc.). Each template includes a ready-to-use system prompt, model configuration, and recommended tool setup. Use templates as starting points when creating new agents.

Get default configuration values for agent creation including timeout settings, max active sessions, max messages, token limits, and intelligence feature flags (sentiment analysis, intent detection, data extraction).
Retrieve a specific agent with full configuration including system prompt, attached model, tools, knowledge stores, voice configuration, phone numbers, and intelligence settings.

patch.description: Update an agent's configuration. Supports partial updates — only include fields you want to change. Updatable fields include system prompt, model, tools, knowledge store, voice configuration, intelligence settings, and avatar.

200.description: The agent has been successfully updated.

delete.description: Permanently delete an agent and its configuration. Existing chat sessions and analytics data are retained but the agent cannot be used for new conversations.

200.description: The agent has been successfully deleted.

delete.summary: Delete a sub-agent mapping (hard delete subtree)

200.description: The tool has been been detached successfully from agent.
The knowledge store is been detached successfully from agent.

400.description: Bad Request. Agent id is required

201.description: Questions Successfully Generated.

post.summary: Generate a MECE resolution taxonomy for the agent

201.description: Generated resolution taxonomy tailored to the agent

200.description: Validation results with scores and findings
The voice config is been detached successfully from agent.

400.description: Bad Request. Agent id is required

post.summary: Clone an existing agent with all its configurations
post.description: Create a duplicate of an agent including system prompt, attached tools, knowledge stores, voice configuration, and intelligence settings. The cloned agent gets a new ID and can be modified independently.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the agent to clone

201.description: The agent has been successfully cloned.

get.summary: Get distinct resolution statuses and sentiments for an agent
get.description: Retrieve list of distinct resolution status values and sentiment values for a specific agent. Useful for populating filter dropdowns.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get distinct options for

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

post.description: Create a new AI agent for a specific domain (call center, support, sales). Requires a system prompt (128-4096 characters) defining the agent behavior, an LLM model ID, and a role. Optionally attach tools, a knowledge store, voice configuration, and intelligence features (sentiment analysis, intent detection).

201.description: The agent has been successfully created.

get.description: List all agents for the authenticated organization with optional filters and pagination. Returns agent details including attached model, tools count, and voice configuration status. Use page and limit query parameters for pagination.

post.description: Validate an agent configuration without creating the agent. Checks system prompt length, model compatibility, tool attachments, and knowledge store integration. Use this before create-agent to catch errors early.

delete.summary: Delete a sub-agent mapping (hard delete subtree)

get.summary: Get public agent details by ID

post.description: Create a voice session for real-time audio conversation with an agent. Requires an agentId with voice enabled and configured STT/TTS models. The session is created on the chat engine side; to initiate the actual voice call pipeline, use the voice API service.

201.description: The voice session has been created successfully.

post.summary: Send a voice message as text

get.summary: Get agent details for voice use

patch.summary: Update analytics data for a chat session
patch.description: Updates status and endedReason for a chat session analytics. Duration is handled by /usage API. Used by voice microservice.

post.description: Create a new chat session for multi-turn conversation with an agent. Requires an agentId. A session persists messages, context, and analytics across multiple message exchanges. Returns a session ID used for sending messages.

201.description: The chat session has been created successfully.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of results per page (defaults to 20)

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent ID to filter by (required when using resolutionStatus or sentiment filters)

200.description: The chat sessions have been retrieved successfully.

post.summary: Send a message to the chat
post.description: Send a user message to an agent and receive a response. Set stream to true for real-time Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming, or stream to false for a complete JSON response with the full agent reply. Non-streaming mode returns { content: AiMessage }. The agent may invoke tools during the response.

get.summary: List all messages of the chat
get.description: Get the full conversation history for a chat session in chronological order. Each message includes role (user, assistant, system, tool), content, timestamp, and any tool call results.

200.description: The messages have been retrieved successfully.
The chat messages has been updated successfully.

get.summary: Get a chat session by ID

200.description: The chat session has been retrieved successfully.
The chat session has been closed successfully.

get.summary: List all Events of the chat

200.description: The Events have been retrieved successfully.

post.summary: List all Events of the chat

200.description: The Events have been retrieved successfully.

get.description: Download chat session data including messages and intelligence analysis as CSV file. If no dates provided, defaults to last 7 days. Maximum date range is 6 months.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to 7 days ago if not provided.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to current date if not provided.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid date range or format

get.summary: Get a chat session by ID (v2)

post.summary: Create a new public chat session

201.description: The chat session has been created successfully.

get.summary: Get a public chat session messages

200.description: The chat session messages were found.

post.summary: Send a message to the chat

200.description: The chat session has been closed successfully.

201.description: The model has been successfully created.

400.description: Invalid request body - must be a non-empty array of models

get.description: List all available AI models grouped by provider. Each provider entry contains a models array — use models[].id (UUID) when calling create-agent or update-agent. Filter by type (text-to-text, text-to-speech, speech-to-text, speech-to-speech) to get only LLM, TTS, STT, or STS models respectively.

200.description: Return all models grouped by provider. Each model has an id (UUID) field.
The model has been successfully updated.
The model has been successfully deleted.

400.description: Bad request. The model cannot be deleted.

200.description: The model pricing has been successfully updated.

get.description: Retrieve analytics for voice calls including active, failed, missed, and total inbound calls

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

get.summary: Get call end reasons for pie chart
get.description: Retrieve call end reasons with counts and percentages for pie chart visualization

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

count.description: Number of calls with this end reason

get.summary: Get call status distribution for pie chart
get.description: Retrieve call status distribution with counts and percentages for pie chart visualization

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

status.description: Call status (e.g., STARTED, COMPLETED, FAILED, MISSED)

count.description: Number of calls with this status

get.summary: Get call duration distribution for line chart
get.description: Retrieve call duration sum over time with dynamic grouping (day/week/month) for line chart visualization

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

period.description: Time period label (date, week, or month)

totalDurationSeconds.description: Total call duration in seconds for this period

callCount.description: Number of calls in this period

get.summary: Get call direction distribution for line chart
get.description: Retrieve inbound and outbound call counts over time with dynamic grouping (day/week/month) for line chart visualization

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

period.description: Time period label (date, week, or month)

inboundCalls.description: Number of inbound calls in this period

outboundCalls.description: Number of outbound calls in this period

get.summary: Get average call duration distribution for line chart
get.description: Retrieve average call duration over time with dynamic grouping (day/week/month) and overall statistics for line chart visualization

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

200.description: Average call duration distribution retrieved successfully

period.description: Time period label (date, week, or month)

averageDurationSeconds.description: Average call duration in seconds for this period

callCount.description: Number of calls in this period

overallAverageDurationSeconds.description: Overall average call duration in seconds across all periods

totalCallCount.description: Total number of calls across all periods

periodType.description: Type of grouping used for the data

get.description: Retrieve comprehensive statistics for a specific agent including average call duration, total call minutes, transfer rate, and First Call Resolution (FCR) rate

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get statistics for

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

get.summary: Get agent resolution outcome distribution for pie chart
get.description: Retrieve resolution outcome distribution with counts and percentages for pie chart visualization. Groups by user-defined resolution statuses.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get resolution outcomes for

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

get.summary: Get agent sentiment distribution for pie chart
get.description: Retrieve sentiment distribution with counts and percentages for pie chart visualization. Shows distribution of positive, negative, and neutral sentiments.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get sentiment distribution for

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

get.summary: Get agent ended reason distribution for pie chart
get.description: Retrieve call ended reason distribution with counts and percentages for pie chart visualization. Shows distribution of agent hangup, user hangup, transferred, etc.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get ended reason distribution for

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the query (ISO string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the query (ISO string)

200.description: Agent ended reason distribution retrieved successfully

get.summary: Get audio analytics for a voice session
get.description: Retrieve comprehensive audio analytics including agent performance, user experience, conversation analysis, and call quality metrics for a specific voice session

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The ID of the chat session to get audio analytics for

404.description: Session not found or not a voice session

post.description:
This creates a new application for configuring webhooks, mainly used for multi tenancy purpose
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

201.description: Application Created

get.description:
This retrives all the application for the webhooks configuation
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

This lists all the endpoints for an application
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

201.description: List of webhook endpoints

post.description:
This creates a new webhook endpoint, mainly used to create endpoint with event mapping
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

201.description: The endpoint has been successfully created.

patch.description:
This updates existing endpointfor an application
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

200.description: The endpoint has been successfully updated.

delete.description:
This deletes an endpoint, this is a soft delete and only marks endpoint as archived: true
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

204.description: The endpoint has been successfully deleted.

get.description:
This lists all the event types in the webhooks configurations
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

post.description:
This creates an event-type for the webhooks, mainly used for introducing new events
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

201.description: The event type has been successfully created.

patch.description:
This updates an existing event type by name
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

200.description: The event type has been successfully updated.

delete.description:
This deletes an event type, this is a soft delete and only marks event as archived: true
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

204.description: The event type has been successfully deleted.

post.description:
This creates a new message with the event type, which broadcasts it to all the endpoints connected to that event type
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

get.description:
This endpoint returns information related to an endpoint, can be used to check the messages sent to the particular endpoint
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

post.description:
Configure webhooks for an organization. Currently supports:
    - Creating new webhook configuration and Svix application
    Note: This is a management API and requires api-key

201.description: Webhook configuration created successfully.

400.description: Invalid configuration type or organization ID already configured.

get.summary: Get Svix application ID for organization
get.description: Retrieves the Svix configs associated with the given organization ID

404.description: No webhook configuration found for the organization

get.description: Get default configuration values for tool creation including available tool types (API_TOOL for HTTP endpoints, FUNCTION_TOOL for LLM-callable functions), parameter validation rules, and authentication schemes.

post.description: Create a tool that agents can use during conversations. Two types are supported: FUNCTION_TOOL (most common) — define a function name, description, and parameter schema that the LLM calls during conversation. Requires: name, description, type="FUNCTION_TOOL", functionTool (with name, description, payload as JSON Schema), and ajvPropertiesSchema (same JSON Schema as payload). API_TOOL — calls external HTTP endpoints. Requires: name, description, type="API_TOOL", apiTool (with url, method, optional body/headers/params), and ajvPropertiesSchema.

value.name: GET_WEATHER_FUNCTION_TEST_FINAL
value.description: This is tools created for Weather type of function type

functionTool.name: get_current_temperature
functionTool.description: Get the current temperature for a specific location

201.description: Tool created successfully.

get.description: List all tools for the authenticated organization. Supports pagination (page, limit) and filtering by tool type (API_TOOL, FUNCTION_TOOL). Returns tool definitions with their attached agent count.
Retrieve a specific tool with its full configuration including type, function/API definition, parameter schemas, and metadata.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the tool to retrieve

patch.description: Update a tool's configuration. Supports partial updates — only include fields you want to change. Changes propagate to all agents using this tool.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the tool to update

delete.description: Delete a tool by ID. The tool will be automatically detached from all agents that were using it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the tool to delete

get.description: Get available subscription plans with features, usage limits, pricing tiers, and trial eligibility. Returns plans configured in Stripe.

post.summary: Create a new call campaign with CSV contacts
post.description: Create an outbound call campaign. Requires an agent, phone number, and a contact list (via CSV upload or inline contactsList). Supports scheduling, concurrency limits, and ongoing campaign mode for continuous dialing.

201.description: The call campaign has been successfully created.

get.description: List all outbound call campaigns for the organization with pagination. Returns campaign name, status, agent, total contacts, and progress.

200.description: The call campaigns have been successfully retrieved.

get.summary: Get a call campaign by id
get.description: Get a call campaign with its individual call results, stats (total, completed, failed, pending), and campaign configuration. Supports pagination for call results.

200.description: The call campaign has been successfully retrieved.

patch.summary: Update a call campaign by id
patch.description: Update a campaign's configuration including name, schedule, concurrency, and status. Supports partial updates.

200.description: The call campaign has been successfully updated.

delete.summary: Delete a call campaign by id
delete.description: Delete a call campaign and its associated call records. Active campaigns should be stopped before deleting.

204.description: The call campaign has been successfully deleted.

post.summary: Import contacts from a Google Sheets URL for preview

200.description: The Google Sheet data has been fetched and parsed into contacts for preview.

get.description: Basic health check endpoint for application liveness probe

503.description: The Health Check is not successful

get.description: Retrieves detailed usage statistics and metrics for the specified time interval

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval for the stats (hour, day, week, month)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the statistics (ISO format)

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the statistics (ISO format)

get.description: Retrieves usage statistics grouped by agents for performance analysis

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval for the summary (hour, day, week, month)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for the summary (ISO format)

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for the summary (ISO format)

get.description: Get overall dashboard statistics including total agents, sessions, messages, average session duration, and key performance indicators. Supports date range filtering.
Get session analysis statistics including session counts, average duration, resolution rates, and sentiment breakdown. Supports filtering by agent, date range, and session type with pagination.
Get conversation volume statistics over time showing daily/hourly session and message counts. Useful for understanding traffic patterns and capacity planning.
Get detailed analysis for a specific session including full sentiment journey, resolution tracking, quality metrics, talk time breakdown, and tool usage summary.
Get call analysis statistics including call volumes, durations, success rates, ended reasons, and trends over time. Supports filtering by agent, date range, and call direction.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: The session id of the interaction

201.description: Phone number created successfully.

patch.summary: Map a phone number to an agent

200.description: Phone number mapped to agent successfully.

post.description: Start an outbound call using either agentId or agentPhoneNumberId. If agentId is provided, it will use the active phone number mapped to that agent.

post.summary: Map an agent to a phone number with type (creates phone number if not exists)

200.description: Agent mapped to phone number successfully.

delete.description: deletes the phone number mapped to agents

post.summary: Get agent mappings for multiple phone numbers
post.description: Returns the agent mappings for a list of phone numbers with pagination

201.description: API key generated successfully

post.description: Revokes an API key making it invalid for future use

400.description: Invalid request or API key not found

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

patch.description: Update an existing API key properties

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: List contacts with pagination, search, and filtering. Contacts are people who interact with agents via chat or voice. Supports filtering by tags, phone number, and external ID.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: The phone number of the contact

200.description: The contacts have been successfully retrieved.

post.description: Create or import a contact with phone number, metadata, and tags. Used for setting up outbound campaigns or pre-registering known callers.

201.description: The contact has been successfully created.

patch.description: Update a contact's metadata, tags, or phone number. Supports partial updates.

200.description: The contact has been successfully updated.

delete.description: Permanently delete a contact by ID. The contact will be removed from any associated campaigns.

204.description: The contact has been successfully deleted.

get.description: Retrieve a list of available voices for each provider
Retrieve a list of available AI models across different providers and categories

post.description:
Create a room token asynchronously through the queueing system.

This endpoint queues the token creation request and returns a job ID
that can be used to poll for the result.
Simulate an inbound telephony/WebRTC call and record it in Redis.
Purchase a new phone number from specified provider.

delete.description: Delete a phone number and associated resources.

post.description:
Initiate an outbound call (SIP or Maqsam API) synchronously.
NOTE: For Maqsam, success only indicates the request was sent.

get.description: Get paginated list of outbound phone numbers with agent id information.
Get a phone number and associated resources.

patch.description: Update phone number to agent mapping.
Allow customers to update per-number concurrency overrides directly in Postgres.

post.description: Queue an outbound phone call initialization (SIP or Maqsam API).
Queue an outbound phone call initialization (SIP or Maqsam API).
Add $2 charge to subscription for specific phone number.

Transfer a sip call

get.description:
Fetch telephony call status for a session.

This endpoint retrieves the current call status from the appropriate telephony provider
using the session ID and call parameters provided as query parameters.

parameters.name: phone_number

schema.description: Phone number used for the call
schema.title: Phone Number

parameters.description: Phone number used for the call

parameters.name: provider_name

schema.description: Telephony provider name (maqsam, twilio, telnyx, etc.)
schema.title: Provider Name

parameters.description: Telephony provider name (maqsam, twilio, telnyx, etc.)

get.description: Health check endpoint.

voice.description: The voice modulation of the assistant

language.description: The language used by assistant to respond back
The language used by assistant to respond back

punctuate.description: Enabling option to punctuate speech text

sampleRate.description: Sample rate (in Hz) for audio processing.

fillerWords.description: Enabling option to disable filler words

keyWords.description: Enabling option to disable key words

keyTerms.description: Enabling option to disable key terms

energyFilter.description: Enabling option to disable energy filter

emotion.description: The voice emotion of the assistant

level.description: The voice emotion level of the assistant

voice.description: The voice modulation of the assistant

instructions.description: The instructions for the TTS assistant

speed.description: The voice speed of the assistant (can be a preset speed or a number)

volume.description: The voice volume of the assistant (can be a preset volume or a number)

language.description: The voice language of the assistant

emotion.description: The voice emotion of the assistant

pitch.description: The voice pitch of the assistant

style.description: Voice style. String for legacy emotion-based providers; number 0.0-1.0 for ElevenLabs style exaggeration.

useSpeakerBoost.description: ElevenLabs speaker boost. Boosts similarity to source voice.

optimizeStreamingLatency.description: ElevenLabs streaming latency optimization (0-4). 0 best quality, 4 lowest latency.

autoMode.description: ElevenLabs auto mode. Reduces latency for complete sentences.

provider.description: Provider for the welcome override model

modelId.description: Model id for the welcome override model

voiceId.description: Voice id (TTS only) for the welcome override

isCustomVoiceEnabled.description: Whether the welcome TTS voice is a custom voice id (UI state)

customVoiceId.description: Custom voice id for the welcome TTS (UI state)

tts.description: TTS override for the greeting; blank fields are derived

llm.description: LLM override for a dynamic greeting; blank fields are derived

minSpeechDuration.description: Minimum duration (in seconds) of speech to consider as valid input for voice activation.

activationThreshold.description: Threshold for VAD sensitivity. Lower value makes VAD more sensitive (detects quieter sounds), higher value makes it less sensitive.

prefixPaddingDuration.description: Duration (in seconds) of silence to add before starting to record voice input.

minSilenceDuration.description: Minimum duration of silence (in seconds) at the end of speech before ending the speech segment.

maxBufferedSpeech.description: Maximum duration (in seconds) of buffered speech input.

sampleRate.description: Sample rate (in Hz) for audio processing.

paddingDuration.description: Duration (in seconds) of silence to pad at the end of voice input.

enabled.description: Indicates whether background noise is enabled.

thinkingVolume.description: Volume (0.0-1.0) of the keyboard-typing thinking sound layered on top of the ambient track during agent thinking pauses.

minSilenceDuration.description: Minimum silence duration before smart turn triggers.

enabled.description: Whether end call functionality is enabled

customMessage.description: Custom message to play when ending the call
customMessage.example: Thank you for calling. Have a great day!

useDynamicMessage.description: Whether to use dynamic message generation for end call

messageMaxWords.description: Maximum number of words for the end call message

delayBeforeHangup.description: Delay in seconds before hanging up after the message

enabled.description: Whether call transfer functionality is enabled

phoneNumber.description: Phone number to transfer the call to

customMessage.description: Custom message to play when transferring the call
customMessage.example: I will transfer you to our specialist now.

errorMessage.description: Error message to play if transfer fails
errorMessage.example: Sorry, I could not transfer your call. Please try again later.

useDynamicMessage.description: Whether to use dynamic message generation for transfer

customMessage.description: Custom message to leave as voicemail
customMessage.example: This is John from ACME Corp. I will call you back soon.

useDynamicMessage.description: Whether to use dynamic message generation for voicemail

messageMaxWords.description: Maximum number of words for the voicemail message

delayAfterMessage.description: Delay in seconds after leaving the message

enabled.description: Whether dynamic transfer routing is enabled

prompt.description: System prompt used to decide routing dynamically
prompt.example: If the user wants to reach support, transfer to +1 (925) 222-2222; if the user wants to reach sales, transfer to +1 (925) 333-3333

welcomeText.description: Welcome text for the voice agent

welcomeMode.description: Welcome mode that determines how the conversation is initiated

welcomePrompt.description: Welcome prompt for dynamic AI-initiated conversations. This field is REQUIRED when welcomeMode is set to "aiInitiatesDynamic".

maxDurationInSeconds.description: Max duration of a voice session

maxActiveSessions.description: Max Active sessions for an agent

maxTimeoutInSeconds.description: Max timeout for an agent in seconds

idleTimeoutInSeconds.description: Max Active sessions for an agent

maxOutputTokens.description: Max llm output tokens for voice

audioIntelligenceEnabled.description: Enable or disable audio intelligence analytics for this agent

handoffMessageConfig.description: Root agent handoff message configuration (stored in voiceConfig.metaData.handoffMessageConfig).

vadConfigs.description: Configuration for Voice Activity Detection (VAD). It defines parameters for detecting speech in audio streams.

allowInterruptions.description: Indicates whether interruptions are allowed during speech detection.

interruptSpeechDuration.description: The duration in milliseconds after which speech interruption is triggered.

interruptMinWords.description: The minimum number of words required for an interruption to occur.

minEndpointingDelay.description: The minimum delay in milliseconds before a speech endpointing is considered valid.

preemptiveSynthesis.description: Indicates whether preemptive synthesis is enabled to start speaking before the full text is generated.

overrideSttModelId.description: STT model ID to use when overrideStt is enabled.

disableInactivityTimeout.description: Indicates whether inactivity timeout for voice is enabled/disabled

functionToolsConfig.description: Configuration for function tools (end call, transfer call, voicemail)

dynamicTransferEnabled.description: Enable dynamic transfer routing for phone calls

dynamicTransferSystemPrompt.description: System prompt to decide dynamic transfer routing
dynamicTransferSystemPrompt.example: If the user wants to reach support, transfer to +1 (925) 222-2222; if the user wants to reach sales, transfer to +1 (925) 333-3333

description.description: Description of what this field represents

choices.description: Available choices (required when type is enum)

examples.description: Example values to help the LLM understand the expected format and content

sentimentAnalysis.description: To enable or disable Sentiment analysis for the agent

intentDetection.description: To enable or disable Intent detection for the agent

resolutionAnalysis.description: To enable or disable Resolution analysis for the agent

resolutionDefinitions.description: Custom resolution definitions with status, descriptions, and classifications. When resolutionAnalysis is enabled, must include at least one SUCCESS and one FAILED classification.

description.description: Description of what this status means

includeConversationSummary.description: To enable or disable conversation summary/history in system prompt

includePredefinedPrompt.description: To enable or disable predefined prompts in the system prompt

dataExtractionEnabled.description: To enable or disable structured data extraction from conversations

dataExtractionSchema.description: Array of field definitions for data extraction from conversations. Similar to Retell AI post-call analysis format.

example.name: issueResolved
example.description: Whether the customer's issue was resolved

fullName.description: The full name of the agent

welcomeText.description: The Welcome message for the agent (2-1000 characters)

systemPrompt.description: The system prompt for the agent

role.description: The Role of the agent, like Du Telecom customer support

voiceEnabled.description: To enable voice feature for an agent

format_prompt.default:
# Multiple Speakers:
- Different unknown speakers are indicated with `<Sn/>` tags.
- Known speakers are indicated with `<Name/>` tags.
- Use the context of the conversation to establish the names of the unknown speakers.
- Do not include `<Sn/>` or `<Name/>` tags in your responses.

VoiceApi_TTSModel.title: TTSModel
VoiceApi_TTSModel.description:
Model for text-to-speech voice configuration.

Attributes
----------
value : str
    The unique identifier for the voice.
label : str
    Human-readable name for the voice.
metadata : Optional[dict]
    Additional provider-specific metadata.
description : Optional[str]
    Detailed description of the voice.
languages : Optional[List[str]]
    List of supported languages.

VoiceApi_TTSVoice.title: TTSVoice
VoiceApi_TTSVoice.description:
Model for text-to-speech voice configuration.

Attributes
----------
value : str
    The unique identifier for the voice.
label : str
    Human-readable name for the voice.
metadata : Optional[dict]
    Additional provider-specific metadata.
description : Optional[str]
    Detailed description of the voice.
gender : Optional[Literal["male", "female", "neutral"]]
    Gender of the voice.
languages : Optional[List[str]]
    List of supported languages.
styles : Optional[List[str] | List[float]]
    List of available voice styles or modulation values.
sample_url : Optional[str]
    URL to a sample of the voice.

VoiceApi_WelcomePipelineConfig-Input.title: WelcomePipelineConfig
VoiceApi_WelcomePipelineConfig-Input.description:
Optional pipeline overrides for the welcome message.

All model fields default to None (NOT a hardcoded model). An unset field
means "inherit from the main agent's config for the current mode" — the
voice-worker resolves it. This avoids baking in a model name (e.g.
gpt-4o-mini) that rots when the model is deprecated. Kept in sync with
agntix-voice-worker's models/config.py.
